description: Guard class definition: movement, perception, alertness ladder, takedown/consciousness, and the personality set. Reference data for Phases 2/4/5 — not runtime-loaded yet. All numbers are Phase 17 tuning surfaces unless noted.

lightLevelFalloff.description: Multiplier on effective vision range per target light level. Shadow keeps the Infiltrator near-invisible at distance.

categoryDistanceFractions.description: Fractions of light-adjusted effective vision range: closer than clear -> clear, closer than partial -> partial, else silhouette.

vision.closeRangeCaptureRule: A guard with visibility 'clear' on the Infiltrator inside closeRangeCaptureDistance captures immediately at any alertness ladder rung. No reaction window.

hearing.rule: Per sound event, per guard: P(notice) = clamp01(loudness * falloff(distance) * alertnessMultiplier). Every event rolls independently; failed rolls are not remembered. Machines (sensors/microphones) never roll — deterministic coverage.

falloff.floorNote: Nowhere is zero — no distance where a loud noise is safe (see DEVELOPMENT.md Phase 2).

alertnessLadder.alarmedRule: Alarmed guards accept no orders and auto-engage/capture on reaching the Infiltrator.

suspicion.description: Suspicion accumulates per second from what the guard perceives, decays in calm. Crossing thresholds climbs the ladder; dropping below (after the decay delay) descends one rung at a time. Alarmed additionally requires current sight of the target (a noise alone never alarms).

duty.description: Duty execution numbers: patrol waypoint following, investigate/search behavior, chase persistence. All Phase 17 tuning surfaces.

memory.note: Rolling per-guard log of personally perceived sight/sound events. Input to report generation (Phases 6-8).

consciousness.noAutomaticDisclosure: Going Down pushes no event to the Warden; only a status check or the wake-up report reveals it.

badge.flagTrigger: Unresponsive confirmed (status check or wake-up report) AND badge missing -> badge ID flagged facility-wide.

personalities.note: Over-reports; half their sightings are nothing. Changes how they talk, never what they perceive or whether they obey.
Under-reports; when they key up, it's real.
Long reports, middling reliability — costs the Warden reading time.

idFormat.rule: Persistent unique display ID per guard, survives duty/alertness reassignment (Phase 4).
